factors.summary: Demand is accelerating in several segments.

evidence.text: Operating revenue (revenue excluding fuel) increased 5% in the first quarter of 2015 as a result of organic growth. Commercial rental performance improved in the first quarter of 2015 as a result of increased North American demand and higher pricing.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating in several segments, supported by increased revenue and higher pricing.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices in some segments.

evidence.text: Commercial rental revenue increased 8% in the first quarter of 2015 reflecting increased North American demand and higher pricing (up 5% in the first quarter of 2015).
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is successfully raising prices, leading to significant revenue growth.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating in certain areas.

evidence.text: Operating revenue (revenue excluding fuel) increased 5% in the first quarter of 2015 as a result of organic growth. Commercial rental performance improved in the first quarter of 2015 as a result of increased North American demand and higher pricing.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by increased demand and higher pricing.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing in key areas.

evidence.text: The average number of full service lease vehicles increased 3% from the prior year, reflecting continued increased sales activity. Commercial rental utilization — power fleet (3) 73.4% 73.6% (20) bps
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing, reflecting increased sales activity and higher utilization.

factors.summary: North American demand is a key growth driver.

evidence.text: Commercial rental performance improved in the first quarter of 2015 as a result of increased North American demand and higher pricing.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: North American demand is a key growth driver, contributing to improved performance.

factors.summary: Margins are improving due to higher revenue and cost management.

evidence.text: FMS EBT increased 17% in the first quarter of 2015 to $89.9 million reflecting strong commercial rental performance, higher full service lease results and increased fuel margins partially offset by one-time benefits in the prior year from lower vehicle service activities due to severe winter weather.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Margins are improving due to strong commercial performance and cost management.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are being managed effectively.

evidence.text: NYE units increased compared to March 31, 2014, due to new lease sales activity. NLE units decreased compared to March 31, 2014 reflecting lower used vehicle inventories.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Inventory levels are being managed effectively, with increases in new lease sales.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange is negatively impacting margins.

evidence.text: Foreign exchange negatively impacted total and operating revenue growth by 100 basis points and 200 basis points, respectively. The decline in total revenue reflects lower subcontracted transportation, lower fuel costs passed through to customers, and a 200 basis point negative impact from foreign exchange.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange is negatively impacting margins, with significant basis point declines.

factors.summary: Management is confident in future growth.

evidence.text: We expect favorable full service lease revenue comparisons to continue through the end of the year based on strong sales activity. We expect favorable commercial rental comparisons to continue throughout the year.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management's confidence is supported by favorable revenue comparisons and strong sales activity.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing to support growth.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures (accrual basis) increased 10% in the three months ended March 31, 2015 to $653.2 million reflecting planned higher investments in the commercial rental fleet. We expect full-year 2015 accrual basis capital expenditures from continuing operations to be $2.63 billion, up from previous expectations of $2.55 billion, to support stronger rental demand.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased capital expenditures indicate support for growth and higher rental demand.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is positive.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities from continuing operations increased to $277.9 million in the three months ended March 31, 2015 compared with $237.7 million in 2014, reflecting higher earnings and lower working capital needs from improved customer collections.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Positive free cash flow generation reflects higher earnings and improved collections.
